reviews.question: What knowledge, methods, skills, insights, or ways of thinking did you develop in this course? Please describe three specific things you learned.
responses: max flow, linear programming, dynamic programming
DP proofs, approaches to DP, reduction of network flow problems
dynamic programming , minmax problem, greedy algorithm
Assuming this section is for discussion: the application of the algorithmic concepts we learned in class, cool solutions to problems we skipped over in lecture, and runtime complexity concepts.
the ability of Mathematical proof

Algorithm, analysis and design.

1. Network flow

2. Greedy algorithms

Working through example problems, approaching new problems with knowledge of similar problems, approaching entirely new problems.
How to formulate real life problems as algorithmic ones, how to develop and algorithm and implement it in Python, how to reduce problems to algorithms already known.
Some more example questions
1. Utilizing various algorithms and fitting them for our needs. 2. New algorithms, such as mst and simplex. 3. Expected time calculation

I thought the discussion helped me learn ways to apply or modify known algorithms to solve a new problem that it wasn't intended to originally solve. Also, it helped me better understand some of the processes behind the algorithms and principles that followed them. They also really helped for the homeworks.
Develping algorithms to solve differnent problems. Analyzing time complexity of algorithms.

reviews.question: Reflecting on the overall learning environment of this class, in what ways did the instructor(s) and the structure or components of the course facilitate your learning? Are there specific course components or methods of instruction you’d keep for future years?
responses: Very clear written notes and clear explanation
Videos online
we got recorded lecture and notes, but having sample test would be better.
The TAs did a great job at facilitating a constructive learning environment.
Good. yes
I think recording those sessions, just like the regular classes are recorded, would be a very good idea.
Homeworks
See first submission.
I think that the instructors did a good job connecting the coursework to the homework in order to ensure that students learned the material well.
I liked that the instructors would give us time during the discussion to work on problems individually before walking us through the answer.
For me, having the notes ahead of time in a pdf was helpful to follow along during the lectures.
Including sample questions is very good.
Lecture slides and textbook
I would keep the homeworks as 2 weeks instead of weekly

Each lecture is good and motivating. But it is better to talk about one concept in more detail for those who cannot understand one new concept quickly. So it is better to reduce the total number of contents to be taught in each lecture. Instead, pay more attention to the concepts (each spent more time to illustrate)

More TA demo, easier exam

More class participation would have been beneficial for my learning experience.
Walking through examples of problems that we talked about in class
I think conceptually the problems are challenging which made it stimulating.
n/a
Challenging but reasonable psets
I think if we moved faster it would have been better for me, but I know that's not practical
Reduce number of exams and homework. Be more lenient in grading. Even participation counts towards grade is better than so many exams and homeworks.
